one_line_ask: Batch-process six raw 925-silver SKU photos into consistent, cast-free, pure-#FFFFFF e-commerce images — isolate each piece, grade the metal so it reads crisp, and deliver a transparent PNG plus a 3000x3000 white-background JPEG per SKU.
brief: Rukmini Silverworks, a boutique 925 sterling-silver house, is re-listing its collection across its own store and two marketplaces and needs clean, consistent product imagery so the metalwork stays the hero. The owner has handed off six raw tabletop frames shot on a phone under mixed studio light — a filigree pendant, a pair of jhumka drop earrings, a stacking band ring, a chain bracelet, an oxidised cuff, and a solitaire-look ring. As-shot they are inconsistent: slightly tilted, framed loose with a grey sweep and prop clutter visible, dull/flat exposure, and a warm-to-cool colour cast that makes the silver look muddy or yellow rather than bright neutral. The job is a batch clean-up, not a re-shoot: for every SKU (1) straighten and level the piece; (2) neutralise the white balance and lift the exposure so the silver reads as bright, cool-neutral metal with no colour cast; (3) gently push clarity/contrast and, on the oxidised piece, hold the dark detail so the antique recesses stay legible; (4) select the jewellery as the subject, invert that selection to a background-only mask, and solid-fill it with pure #FFFFFF so each piece sits on a flat, shadow-free white backdrop (the metalwor
